The Silent Crisis: Child and Youth Deaths Worldwide

Author

Aryan Garg

Published

April 14, 2026

Introduction

Every year, millions of children and young people aged 5–24 die from preventable causes. This report explores the relationship between early childhood care, GDP per capita, and youth mortality across the world.

Key questions we explore:

  • Which regions have the highest youth deaths?
  • Does a country’s wealth affect child survival?
  • How has the situation changed over time?
Code 
import polars as pl
import pandas as pd

# Load the three data files
indicator1 = pl.read_csv("unicef_indicator_1.csv")
indicator2 = pl.read_csv("unicef_indicator_2.csv")
metadata = pl.read_csv("unicef_metadata.csv")

# Filter indicator1 to Total sex only (removes Male/Female duplicates)
ind1_total = indicator1.filter(pl.col("sex") == "Total")

# Filter indicator2 to Total sex only
ind2_total = indicator2.filter(pl.col("sex") == "Total")

# Merge indicator2 with metadata using country name
merged = ind2_total.join(
    metadata.select(["country", "year", "GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)", 
                     "Life expectancy at birth, total (years)"]),
    left_on=["country", "time_period"],
    right_on=["country", "year"],
    how="left"
)

print(ind1_total.head(3))
print(ind2_total.head(3))

Code 
import geopandas as gpd
from plotnine import *
import pandas as pd
import polars as pl

# Convert to pandas for geopandas merge
ind1_pd = ind1_total.to_pandas()

# Load world shapefile (built into geopandas)
world = gpd.read_file("https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/110m/cultural/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.zip")
# Merge map data with indicator data
world_merged = world.merge(
    ind1_pd[["alpha_3_code","obs_value"]],
    left_on="ISO_A3_EH",
    right_on="alpha_3_code",
    how="left"
)

# Plot the map
(
  ggplot(world_merged)
  + geom_map(aes(fill="obs_value"), color="white", size=0.2)
  + scale_fill_gradient(low="#FAECE7", high="#1e59d0", na_value="#79e8db",
                        name="% children\nwith care")
  + coord_equal()
  + theme_void()
  + theme(figure_size=(10, 5),
          plot_title=element_text(size=12, weight="bold"),
          legend_position="right")
  + labs(title="Early Childhood Care Coverage Worldwide")
)

Where Are Youth Deaths Concentrated?

Improving early childhood care can save lives. The map above shows the percentage of children receiving early stimulation. But where are youth deaths most concentrated? The bar chart below highlights the top 10 countries with the highest youth deaths (ages 5–24) in the most recent year available.

Code 
# Get most recent year per country, sum male+female
recent = (
    ind2_total
    .filter(pl.col("sex") == "Total")
    .group_by("country")
    .agg(pl.col("obs_value").last().alias("deaths"),
         pl.col("time_period").last().alias("year"))
    .sort("deaths", descending=True)
    .head(10)
    .to_pandas()
)

(
  ggplot(recent, aes(x="reorder(country, deaths)", y="deaths", fill="deaths"))
  + geom_bar(stat="identity", show_legend=False)
  + coord_flip()
  + scale_fill_gradient(low="#9FE1CB", high="#0F6E56")
  + scale_y_continuous(labels=lambda l: [f"{v/1000:.0f}k" for v in l])
  + theme_minimal()
  + theme(figure_size=(8, 5),
          axis_title=element_text(size=10),
          plot_title=element_text(size=12, weight="bold"))
  + labs(title="Top 10 Countries: Youth Deaths (Ages 5–24)",
         x="Country", y="Number of Deaths")
)

Wealth and Child Survival

Richer countries tend to have better healthcare. The chart below explores whether GDP per capita predicts youth deaths.

Code 
scatter_df = (
    merged
    .filter(
        pl.col("GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)").is_not_null() &
        pl.col("obs_value").is_not_null()
    )
    .group_by("country")
    .agg([
        pl.col("GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)").mean().alias("gdp"),
        pl.col("obs_value").mean().alias("deaths")
    ])
    .to_pandas()
)

(
  ggplot(scatter_df, aes(x="gdp", y="deaths"))
  + geom_point(color="#185FA5", alpha=0.5, size=2)
  + geom_smooth(method="lm", color="#D85A30", se=True)
  + scale_x_log10(labels=lambda l: [f"${v/1000:.0f}k" for v in l])
  + scale_y_continuous(labels=lambda l: [f"{v/1000:.0f}k" for v in l])
  + theme_minimal()
  + theme(figure_size=(8, 5),
          plot_title=element_text(size=12, weight="bold"))
  + labs(title="Higher GDP Is Associated with Fewer Youth Deaths",
         x="GDP per capita (log scale, constant 2015 USD)",
         y="Average youth deaths (ages 5–24)")
)

Progress Over Time

Code 
countries_to_show = ["India", "Nigeria", "Brazil", "China", "Ethiopia"]

ts_df = (
    ind2_total
    .filter(pl.col("country").is_in(countries_to_show))
    .filter(pl.col("sex") == "Total")
    .to_pandas()
)

(
  ggplot(ts_df, aes(x="time_period", y="obs_value", color="country"))
  + geom_line(size=1)
  + geom_point(size=1.5)
  + scale_y_continuous(labels=lambda l: [f"{v/1000:.0f}k" for v in l])
  + scale_color_manual(values=["#185FA5","#D85A30","#1D9E75","#D4537E","#BA7517"])
  + theme_minimal()
  + theme(figure_size=(9, 5),
          plot_title=element_text(size=12, weight="bold"),
          legend_title=element_blank())
  + labs(title="Youth Deaths Are Declining — But Unevenly",
         x="Year", y="Deaths (ages 5–24)")
)
